title: Mandatory verification before restricted-account recovery
trigger: Customer asks to unblock or restore account access while verification is pending or required.
allowed_actions: Explain that account restrictions depend on verification completion.
Request missing verification documents or direct the customer to the verification flow.
Route unclear or disputed cases to the verification team.
prohibited_actions: Promise account unblocking before verification review is complete.
Share internal investigation details.
Bypass mandatory verification because the customer is upset or high value.

title: Delayed deposit evidence gate
trigger: Customer reports that a deposit has not appeared.
allowed_actions: Check elapsed time, payment method, amount, and transaction status.
If the normal timeframe has not passed, explain the wait window.
If the timeframe has passed, request successful payment proof and create or update a financial-operations task.
prohibited_actions: Confirm funds were received without payment evidence.
Blame a payment provider without evidence.
Promise an exact credit time without financial-operations confirmation.

title: Processed withdrawal not received proof gate
trigger: Customer says a withdrawal is marked processed but funds are not visible in their account.
allowed_actions: Check processed date, payment method, and method-specific settlement window.
Request a bank statement or official bank response when the waiting window has passed.
Escalate with proof if a tracking reference cannot locate the funds.
prohibited_actions: Tell the customer the withdrawal failed if the system says processed.
Guarantee reversal or instant credit.
Expose internal payment-limit calculations.

title: Withdrawal limits are not disclosed
trigger: Customer asks for the exact withdrawal limit, limit logic, or why a payment method is unavailable.
allowed_actions: Explain that available methods are shown in the withdrawal section.
Suggest using an available payment method.
Route exceptions to financial operations when the customer provides evidence of a valid issue.
prohibited_actions: Disclose internal withdrawal-limit amount or calculation logic.
Promise a limit increase.
Advise customers to bypass controls.

title: Bonus funds and promo code claims require account evidence
trigger: Customer asks about bonus availability, removal, turnover, withdrawal impact, or applying a new promo code.
allowed_actions: Explain general bonus mechanics.
Check active bonus status before describing account-specific availability.
Clarify that removing a bonus may affect virtual funds and that new offers depend on available promo codes.
prohibited_actions: Promise a bonus will be granted.
Guarantee a promo code is available without account evidence.
Hide withdrawal or turnover implications.

title: Cross-account disclosure is prohibited
trigger: Customer contacts support from one email or account and asks about another account.
allowed_actions: Explain that account-specific details can only be shared through the registered contact channel.
Ask the customer to contact support from the registered email or complete identity verification.
Route suspicious access patterns to account security.
prohibited_actions: Share another account's details.
Merge or change account identity without verification.
Send sensitive account status to an unverified contact.

title: Channel tone and closure discipline
allowed_actions: Acknowledge the concern in the customer's channel and language.
State the next action and expected follow-up path.
Close only after confirming resolution or explaining the pending next step.
prohibited_actions: Use unsupported certainty.
Send generic template language that ignores the customer's issue.
Close a live chat without giving the customer a clear next step.

title: Priority support does not bypass policy
trigger: High-touch or priority support customer asks for account, payment, bonus, verification, or exception handling.
allowed_actions: Use stronger empathy and faster routing.
Escalate with clear ownership and evidence.
Keep all verification, payment, and disclosure rules active.
prohibited_actions: Override policy because the customer is priority.
Promise an exception before the owning team approves it.
Reveal internal review or limit logic.
